message:
fix(gitlab): trim whitespace from custom base URLs

GitLabProvider::with_base_url() now trims leading/trailing whitespace
from the provided URL string before checking emptiness and storing it.
Previously, a URL like " https://gitlab.example.com/ " would be stored
with whitespace intact, producing malformed API endpoints like
" https://gitlab.example.com/ /api/v4/chat/completions" when
chat_url() concatenated the path.

Also handles the edge case where a whitespace-only string (e.g. "   ")
is now correctly treated as empty and falls back to the default GitLab
base URL, matching the existing behavior for empty strings.

Adds two tests:
- test_gitlab_whitespace_base_url_uses_default: verifies whitespace-only
  input falls back to DEFAULT_GITLAB_BASE
- test_gitlab_base_url_is_trimmed: verifies padded URLs are trimmed and
  chat_url() produces correct endpoint paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
